arcana.label: Arcana
arcana.desc: A spellcaster's reserves. Scrolls, reagents, and arcane curiosities.

apothecary.label: Apothecary
apothecary.desc: A healer's stock or alchemist's shelf. Potions, elixirs, and medicinal supplies.

armaments.label: Armaments
armaments.desc: Military surplus or a warrior's stash.

implements.label: Implements
implements.desc: A crafter's workshop or cult supplies.

relics.label: Relics
relics.desc: Remnants of the dead or divine.

abandoned.label: Abandoned
abandoned.desc: Forgotten junk. Mostly worthless, occasionally surprising.

dungeon: Prising apart a loose floor tile, you find {container} concealed in the hollow beneath.
Wedged behind a supporting pillar and half-buried in rubble -- {container}.
A section of wall gives under pressure. In the shallow recess behind it, someone left this.
Scratched into the floor is a crude arrow. Following it leads to a collapsed niche.
The cell has a false bottom. It comes away cleanly. Whoever hid this knew what they were doing.
The skeleton in the alcove had its arms folded over something. It takes a moment to see why.
forest: Between the thick roots of an old oak, pressed flat under leaf litter -- {container}.
The mossy stump is hollow. Someone used it as a cache, and not long ago.
Bark stripped from a birch reveals a recess sealed with pine resin. Still dry inside.
A rock that doesn't sit right. The soil beneath is freshly turned.
Caught in a hollow branch just above eye level, a bundle wrapped in oilskin.
Pressed flat under a sheet of fallen bark -- {container}. Easy to miss.
desert: Beneath an overturned stone, shaded from the sun -- {container}.
The cairn was built to mark something. Beneath the lowest stone, wrapped in cloth, this.
A scraped furrow in the sand, half-filled by the wind. Whatever buried this wasn't expecting to leave it long.
Behind a wind-carved pillar of sandstone, in the cool shadow -- {container}.
The dry riverbed has a false bottom here. An old trader's trick.
Tucked under the lip of a rock shelf, just out of the sun. Easy to miss against the stone.
jungle: The root system of a banyan has grown around {container}. It takes some effort to free.
A hollow bamboo stalk, capped and lashed. Whoever made it knew how to keep water out.
Someone wove the entrance shut with green vines. Careful cutting reveals {container} behind them.
Buried at the base of a strangler fig, deep enough to stay cool through the heat.
The canopy drip carved a channel in the stone over the years. Following it leads to a dry pocket.
A flat stone half-sunken into the mud. Beneath it, wrapped in broad leaves, something was set aside.
urban: A loose brick in the alley wall. The mortar around it is barely set.
Under a market stall's false floor, sealed with a cloth rag. The stall has been empty for weeks.
A locked strongbox beneath a floorboard. The key was around the last occupant's neck.
Stuffed inside a chimney breast, wrapped in wax paper against the soot.
The cellar drain runs deeper than it should. A bundle is wedged into the bend.
Behind a panel in the wainscoting, the wood slightly proud of the wall if you know to look.
coastal: Jammed into a rock crevice above the tideline, sealed in a waterproofed oilcloth roll.
The sea-cave narrows at the back. A ledge, invisible at high tide, holds this.
Beneath a cairn of wave-smoothed stones on the strand.
Lashed to the underside of a pier strut, just above the high-water mark.
{container}, wedged in a hull breach. The seal held. Just.
The hollow in the driftwood is deeper than it looks. Oilcloth inside, still intact.
mountain: Wedged into a crack in the cliff face, barely visible from below.
Beneath a cairn at the ridge line, the stones placed with purpose.
The mountain hut's floorboards are loose. Someone used the crawlspace.
A narrow chimney in the rock face. The opening at the top conceals {container}.
The goat trail ends at a shelf. Someone camped here, and left something behind.
Ice-crusted and half-buried in scree. The oilcloth wrapping saved it.
